issues.description: Glob `/cache/*` is not expanded without a shell. `pods.exec(pod_name, vec!["du", "-sh", "/cache/*"], &ap)` passes arguments directly to the kernel (no shell), so `*` is treated as a literal filename, not a glob. `du` will fail with "No such file or directory" for `/cache/*`.

The `total` field is set to `format!("{} subdirectories", subdirectories.len())` — a count, not a size. FR-6a specifies `Disk usage: 2.1 GiB / 50 GiB (4%)` with an actual size total. The CLI also formats this as a plain label, so the user never sees actual total usage.
issues.suggestion: Compute total from `du -sh /cache` (without `*`) or sum the subdirectory sizes. Store an actual size string like `"2.1G"` in `total`.

issues.description: FR-6a output format requires a `Cache volume: nautiloop-cache (50 GiB)` line showing PVC name and capacity, and a `Disk usage: X / Y (Z%)` summary line. The CLI output omits both — it has no volume info at all. The `CacheResponse` struct also lacks `volume_name` and `volume_capacity` fields, so the API endpoint doesn't return this data.
issues.suggestion: Add `volume_name: String` and `volume_capacity_gi: Option<u64>` to `CacheResponse`. In the API handler, read PVC `status.capacity` via the kube API. In the CLI, print the volume line and compute the usage percentage.

issues.description: The stdout reader uses a single `read()` call which may return a partial result if the kernel hasn't flushed all output yet. For `du` across many subdirectories the output could exceed one read's worth, truncating the result silently.
issues.suggestion: Use `read_to_end()` or loop on `read()` until EOF within the timeout, e.g. `tokio::io::AsyncReadExt::read_to_string`.

issues.description: AttachParams uses `.stderr(true)` but stderr is never consumed. On kube-rs, unconsumed streams can cause the exec to hang or leak. Additionally, stderr output (e.g. `du: cannot access...`) is silently lost, losing diagnostic info.
issues.suggestion: Either set `.stderr(false)` if you don't need it, or consume stderr to avoid potential hangs and log any errors.

issues.description: When `disk_usage` is `Some` but `subdirectories` is empty, the code skips the disk usage section entirely — no output at all. The user sees no indication of disk usage status, which is confusing. An empty PVC should still show something like `Disk usage: 0 B`.
issues.suggestion: Print a message like `Disk usage: empty (no cache subdirectories)` when subdirectories is empty.

issues.description: The spec's FR-3b implementation note explicitly says `cache` field on `NautiloopConfig` must use `Option<CacheConfig>` (not `#[serde(default)]`). The code uses `#[serde(default)]` on the `Option<CacheConfig>` field. While functionally equivalent for Option types (missing = None in both cases), it contradicts the explicit spec instruction. More importantly, the doc comment on the field itself says 'Uses `Option` (not `#[serde(default)]`)' which is self-contradictory with the actual attribute.
issues.suggestion: Remove `#[serde(default)]` from the `cache` field. For `Option` fields, serde already deserializes missing keys as `None` by default when the container uses `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]` is NOT set — which is the case here. Alternatively, if serde complains, keep the attribute but fix the doc comment.

issues.description: `Api::namespaced(client.clone(), namespace)` clones the client unnecessarily. kube::Client is an Arc wrapper and Clone is cheap, but the function already takes a reference — no issue, just noting the clone happens.
issues.suggestion: No change needed, just a note.

issues.description: The `for (path, size) in &dirs` iterates over references to references (`&&(String, String)`). Works but slightly awkward.
issues.suggestion: Use `for (path, size) in dirs` (consume the vec) since it's not used afterward.
